Broadcom consolidation.  We are seeing a resurgence of Broadcom with VCF 9 and our backoffice expertise is extensive.  Does it make sense to leverage that more so than trying to establish a multi-hypervisor approach.  Could this even be used across more portfolio companies?

We want to accelerate our growth in the UK and does it make sense to consolidate our efforts over there into one team

We've lock in storage pricing for the next 4 years PLUS we have about 8,000 hard drives that would be really strong object storage platform that we could collectively leverage include vendor co-selling

items.subject: Sales Forecast & Pipeline - April 17th Update
items.body_text:
[Tom Kelsch]
Hi team,

The following is a summary of the Sales Forecast & Pipeline for Q3. Changes from last week's figures are noted. All figures in CAD.

Notable changes to opportunities in the past week are detailed below. The reductions to Q3 open pipeline & forecast are largely driven by two losses (Technicost, Ayzenberg) and several large opps being pushed (WEBNX, Great Circle, NetIntegrity, Cofomo).

Closed Won

No notable wins

Closed Lost

Les Systemes De Gestions Technicost - $37K MRC, $15K NRC - New Logo, Managed Services, Q3

Ayzenberg Group - $12K MRC - New Logo, Colocation, Q3

Newly Created

?eské Radiokomunikace - $11K MRC - New Logo, SaaS, Q4

Close Date Pushed

WEBNX Inc - $100K MRC - New Logo, Colocation, pushed from April to June

Great-Circle Technology - $47K MRC, $13K NRC - New Logo, Colocation, pushed from May to July

AC Foods - $47K MRC, $78K NRC - New Logo, PS/MS, pushed from June to August

Lokker - $12K MRC, $53K NRC - New Logo, MS/PS, pushed from April to May

Townsuite Municipal Software - $10K MRC - New Logo, Hosting, pushed from May to July

Inspirata Canada - $8K MRC - New Logo, Hosting, pushed from May to June

NetIntegrity - $100K NRC - Install Base Migration, Hyperscale, pushed from May to October

Cofomo - $63K NRC - Install Base, PS, pushed from April to September

***

To see performance against targets, opportunity details, or other sales analysis please refer to the Power BI Dashboards (updated daily):

Sales Forecasting & Analysis - Power BI Dashboards

Thanks,

Tom Kelsch
Senior Decision Support Analyst
aptum.com | LinkedIn

items.subject: FW: DigitalBridge Incident Escalation Policy - DRAFT
items.body_text:
[Matthew Carter]
Hi Team,
DB is rolling out a policy that requires PortCo’s to identify and report major incidents quickly and create strong governance across companies. This draft will be finalized in May/June but wanted to flag to your attention. 

What must be reported: Anything with serious harm, regulatory/legal risk, reputational damage, or >$1M financial impact across areas like:
Safety, environment, cyber, financial fraud.
Operational failures, outages.
Legal/regulatory issues, litigation.
Leadership changes, misconduct, criminal activity. 
 
Timing:
24 hrs: Initial notification.
72 hrs: Full report.
 
Notification (send to matthew.carter@aptum.com for coordination): PortCo CEO, Board, and DigitalBridge (PortfolioReporting@digitalbridge.com) 
 
Requirements on p15:
State facts (no speculation) 
Assess impact + regulatory status 
Take immediate action 
Investigate root cause + fix 
 
Thanks, 
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

As you know, Aptum is now working with an SFDC vendor who supports us with administrative work, projects, and platform improvements. The first phase focused primarily on cleaning up our SFDC instance, as there were many unused objects and code components. This phase was completed last week.

We have now moved into what is referred to as Camp Services. These are tasks typically handled by a standard SFDC admin and are included in our monthly fee. Any work outside of Camp Services is estimated using points, based on the vendor’s guidelines.

For each engagement, the first step is to confirm whether the request is included in Camp Services. If it is not, I ask the vendor to provide a point estimate for the specific request so we can avoid any unexpected charges.

Improvement
Owner
Comments

Identify the impact of our advisory efforts on future customer acquisition/growth

Look into the current process where renewal opportunities are automatically created and decide if it makes sense

Process to identify and work open opportunities where the close date is in the past

Improvements found in UAT

Remove unused columns from objects
Jorge
This is an ongoing task I will work on.

items.body_text:
[Elizabeth Perez]
Hi All,

Just wanted to provide an update to everyone here.

The customer has been made aware that they are compliant and can continue using the SQL Server + CALs.  We have a call scheduled with the customer on Monday, April 20, to ensure they have the proper number of CALs and to provide guidance should they need to purchase more.

With regard to the legal letter, it's not a hard requirement but is this something we want to have on record just in case? If so, is there anyone in legal that could draft this document? 

Thank you in advance for your time and attention here. 

Elizabeth Perez
Inside Account Manager
+1 (210) 441-4686
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>
Sent: Friday, April 10, 2026 5:35 AM
To: Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>; Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com>
Cc: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification 

Hi All,

Apologies for the delay, I'm ill and I think Jason has also been out.

Samar has confirmed that we do not need SA in this specific scenario, and during our next SPLA audit we should supply the MS auditor with the following:

Invoices of their SQL server and CAL license purchases (I think we have these already).

A signed legal letter that states that the customer knows they are responsible for making sure they have the correct number of SQL CALs, and are liable for any and all fines that Microsoft make on their behalf. I don't think this is strictly needed as Samar from Insight said "they won't chase you for this", but she said it would be good to have. Anyone we can get from legal to do this?
The only other concern is that they have only purchased 1x User/Device CAL for each server. The licensing problem involves the product key used to install SQL Server.  Microsoft gives the exact same product key to all Software Assurance (Enterprise)/MSDN (Developer)/SPLA (Hosting) customers.  Only retail product keys are unique.  Thus, in most cases, the product key is not proof of licensing.  In past audits, if Microsoft found this product key used for SQL Server, it assumed we provided the product key and thus licensing.  If we weren’t sending Microsoft their royalty for the license, we owed back licensing fees.
 
SQL Server + CAL is a retail-only product.  It is, to my knowledge, not offered under Software Assurance, nor can it offered under SPLA.
 
If a customer buys their own SQL Server licenses through Software Assurance, they have the ability to use these licenses in Azure, other public cloud, or a hosting provider.  This is done through License Mobility and requires the license holder to submit a form attesting that they have the required license.
 
Microsoft licensing is complex and my understanding is not complete.  However, since it carries a real financial liability if we get it wrong, I believe it’s important that we understand what Microsoft allows and requires.  I’ve documents that I’ve used to make sense of this.
